item.title: Pentagon identifies 4th service member killed as U.S.-Iran war escalates
item.description: The U.S. launched another night of strikes on Iran after four U.S. soldiers were killed in Jordan and Iraq in recent days.

item.title: 3 soldiers killed in Iran strike on base in Jordan were from Texas, Hawaii, N.Y.
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of three U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens, threatening Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

item.title: Iran war has cost $37.5 billion, Hegseth says, as conflict escalates
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the administration estimates that the Iran war has cost $37.5 billion since it began in late February.

item.title: Storms bring flash flooding across Northeast in "dangerous and life-threatening situation"
item.description: Storms rocked the most populated area of the country on Tuesday, causing widespread flash flooding from Ohio to New England and beyond in what the National Weather Service called a "dangerous and life-threatening situation." Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: RFK Jr. says cyclospora outbreak is "under control"
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is defending his department's handling of the cyclospora outbreak that's sickened thousands of people across the country. Kennedy says the outbreak is "under control." CBS News' Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Democratic lawmakers slam Trump "vanity projects" in shadow hearing
item.description: Lawmakers hammered the president for bulldozing norms in pursuit of his construction goals and imposing his likeness on buildings, coins and passports.

item.title: Here's Tropical Storm Bertha's projected path
item.description: CBS News New York chief weathercaster Lonnie Quinn breaks down where Tropical Storm Bertha is expected to track.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthening as Gulf Coast braces for flooding
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is churning in the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf Coast braces for flooding and potentially deadly storm surge. CBS News' Lana Zak reports and meteorologist Lonnie Quinn has a look at the storm's path.

item.title: Mexican man killed by truck after ICE encounter was on vacation, officials say
item.description: Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán, 28, died July 14 after authorities said he darted across a busy road in St. Augustine, Florida, and into the path of a truck.

item.title: Here's how much wealth you need to thrive, based on your age
item.description: Three in four American households lack the wealth to weather financial setbacks and achieve major life milestones.

item.title: Analyzing Democrats' fundraising gap with Republicans heading into midterms
item.description: With the November midterm elections less than four months away, the Republican Party finds itself with a lot more cash on hand than the Democrats. Political strategists Kurt Bardella and Erin Maguire join with analysis.

item.title: Justice Department monitoring polling sites in 3 Arizona counties
item.description: The Justice Department announced Tuesday that it is monitoring polling sites in three Arizona counties as voters cast ballots in the state's primary elections. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small breaks down the races we're watching.

item.title: Watch: Sen. Peters calls Hegseth a "failure" over Iran war
item.description: Sen. Gary Peters and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth got into a heated exchange on the Senate floor Tuesday with Peters calling Hegseth a failure over the Iran war as the Pentagon seeks billions in additional funding. See the moment.

item.title: Former FDA commissioner unpacks state of cyclospora outbreak as RFK Jr. says it's "under control"
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said on Tuesday the outbreak of a severe intestinal illness is under control, adding the source has been identified. Former FDA Commissioner Dr. Robert Califf joins to assess the federal government's response.

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley says he's "very unlikely to support" GOP budget reconciliation bill
item.description: House Republicans scored a win Tuesday afternoon after the chamber narrowly passed a procedural vote on their legislative agenda. It faces a final vote later in the evening. Independent Rep. Kevin Kiley of California, who typically votes with Republicans, joins "The Takeout" with his perspective.

item.title: Trump eyes attacks on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain as Pentagon seeks more funding
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said the U.S. is not leaving Iran and intends to inflict still more aerial damage on targets, specifically a place called Pickaxe Mountain, but the war will soon run into genuine budget limits. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Weijia Jiang report. Then, Matthew Kroenig, nonresident senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, joins with analysis.

item.title: Hegseth tells senators Iran war has cost $37.5 billion so far
item.description: Senators on Tuesday grilled Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine over the state of the war with Iran. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns has the details.

item.title: DOJ says it's no longer illegal to download TikTok on federal devices
item.description: The Justice Department determined this week a federal law banning TikTok from government devices no longer applies to the social video app.

item.title: Universities get funding from foreign entities on U.S. watch lists, records show
item.description: The State Department is cracking down on universities accepting funding from foreign entities on U S. government watch lists.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine's top general after days of protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues.

item.title: Ex-U.S. attorney in Seattle sues Trump over firing
item.description: Roger Rogoff was fired from his role as U.S. attorney in Seattle shortly after judges on the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington appointed him to the job.

item.title: More Americans report shoplifting as inflation climbs, survey finds
item.description: Thirty percent of respondents said they shoplifted this year, with most citing affordability concerns and financial hardship.

item.title: Kaylee Hottle, actress in "Godzilla vs. Kong," dies at 18 in car crash
item.description: Kaylee Hottle, an Atlanta-born actress known for her role in the "Godzilla" film franchise, has died following a crash in Maryland.

item.title: GLP-1s are linked to a 17% drop in worker sick leave, new study finds
item.description: Here's how weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy could reduce worker illness and save companies money.

item.title: Cocaine worth $285 million found hidden in banana shipment
item.description: Some 1,700 pounds of cocaine was concealed in a refrigerated container from Ecuador among dozens of pallets of bananas, police said.

item.title: Man attacked by bison recalls terrifying encounter: "He could have gored me"
item.description: Carl Isom-McDaniel was on a walk with his grandson at Yellowstone National Park earlier this month when a bison suddenly charged at him and threw him 8 feet into the air.

item.title: Deadly attack on U.S. forces in Jordan part of evolving Iranian tactics
item.description: A retired Jordanian general tells CBS News Iran is "developing techniques and capacities" to target U.S. forces with increasing lethality.

item.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, accusing it of false advertising
item.description: The lawsuit alleges that Eli Lilly misled consumers by using outdated clinical trial data to make its drugs appear more effective than Novo's.
